GREEN.headline: You can likely manage this at home for now.
steps: Rest, stay hydrated, and monitor how you feel.
Use simple home care appropriate to your symptoms.
GREEN.escalateIf: If symptoms get worse, last longer than expected, or new symptoms appear, visit a clinic or doctor.

YELLOW.headline: It's best to see a health worker soon.
steps: Visit a Primary Health Centre (PHC), clinic, or pharmacy/doctor within the next 24–48 hours.
Write down your symptoms, when they started, and anything that makes them better or worse.
Take any current medications and their packs with you.
YELLOW.escalateIf: If your condition worsens quickly or any emergency danger sign appears, go to the nearest hospital immediately.

RED.headline: This may be an emergency – get help now.
steps: Go to the nearest hospital or general hospital emergency department immediately.
If you can, arrange fast, safe transport or ask someone to help you get there.
Do not wait to see if it improves on its own.
RED.escalateIf: If the person becomes unresponsive, stops breathing, or is rapidly worsening, seek the fastest possible emergency help.
